## The Matrix: Worked Example

Let `T = 3`, `d = 4`, `H = 2` heads, so $d_k = d_v = 2$.

Input:

```text
X = [[1, 0, 1, 0],
     [0, 1, 0, 1],
     [1, 1, 0, 0]]   (3×4)
```

*Head 1* uses the first 2 dimensions primarily.

```text
Wq¹ = Wk¹ = Wv¹ = [[1,0],[0,1],[0,0],[0,0]]   (4×2)

Q¹ = X Wq¹ = [[1,0],[0,1],[1,1]]   (3×2)
```

Scores: $S^1 = Q^1{K^1}^{\top} / \sqrt{2}$:

```text
Q¹K¹ᵀ = [[1,0,1],[0,1,1],[1,1,2]]
S¹    = [[0.71, 0.00, 0.71],
         [0.00, 0.71, 0.71],
         [0.71, 0.71, 1.41]]
```

After causal mask and softmax:

```text
A¹ = [[1.000, 0.000, 0.000],
      [0.414, 0.586, 0.000],
      [0.221, 0.221, 0.558]]

head¹ = A¹ V¹ = [[1.000, 0.000],
                 [0.586, 0.414],
                 [0.779, 0.779]]    (3×2)
```

*Head 2* focuses on last 2 dimensions, producing similarly shaped output.
After concatenating both heads and applying the output projection $W_o \in \mathbb{R}^{4\times 4}$:

```text
MultiHead = [head¹ ‖ head²] =
  [[1.000, 0.000, 1.000, 0.000],
   [0.586, 0.414, 0.500, 0.500],
   [0.779, 0.779, 0.421, 0.211]]   (3×4)
```

Figure @fig-ch08-matrix-mha shows parallel attention heads whose outputs are concatenated and projected back to model width.

![Multi-head attention](images/ch08_mha.png){#fig-ch08-matrix-mha fig-alt="Multi-head attention — H parallel heads each attend independently, then concat + project."}
